title: Experimental_Investigation_of_the_Nonlinear_Response_of_Swirl-Stabilized_Flames_
text:
[VISUAL/EQUATION CONTENT - Page 3]
Here's the requested technical content from the provided text:

**1. Mathematical Equations:**

*   (Phi'/Phi) = (m'f/mf) / (1 + m'a/ma)
*   H(f,A) = (Q'/Q) / (Phi'/Phi)

**2. Graphs/Charts:**

NO_VISUAL_CONTENT

**3. Tables:**

NO_VISUAL_CONTENT

**4. Diagrams:**

NO_VISUAL_CONTENT

**5. Key Numbers, Constants, Parameters:**

* Sampling rate: 8192 Hz
*   Frequency resolution: 0.5 Hz
*   Time resolution: 0.122 ms
*   Mean pressure: 1 atm
*   Mean equivalence ratios: 0.55 to 0.65
*   Mean velocity: 60 m/s
*   Inlet temperature: 200°C
*   Reynolds number: Approximately 33,000
* Fuel modulation frequencies: 100 Hz to 400 Hz (Delta f = 25 Hz)
*   Wavelength of He-Ne laser: 3.39 μm.
* Lenses focal length: LS1=533 mm, LS2=51 mm
* Sensing area diameters: 2 mm

title: Experimental_Investigation_of_the_Nonlinear_Response_of_Swirl-Stabilized_Flames_
text:
[VISUAL/EQUATION CONTENT - Page 4]
Here's a breakdown of the technical content extracted from the provided image:

**1. Mathematical Equations:**

*   None explicitly shown

**2. Graphs/Charts:**

***Figure 3(a) and 5(a): Normalized Chemiluminescence Intensity and Velocity vs. Normalized Equivalence Ratio Fluctuation***
    *   **X-axis:** Φ'/Φmean (unitless)
    *   **Y-axis (left):** CH*'/CH*mean (unitless)
    *   **Y-axis (right):** V'/Vmean	(unitless)
    *   **Curves:**
        *   Filled squares: CH*'/CH*mean
        *   Circles: V'/Vmean
    * Key values shown: Blowoff regime

***Figure 3(b) and 5(b): Phase vs. Normalized Equivalence Ratio Fluctuation***
    *   **X-axis:** Φ'/Φmean (unitless)
    *   **Y-axis:** Phase (degrees)
    *   **Curves:** Scatter Plot approximated by a function

**3. Tables:**

*   NO_VISUAL_CONTENT

**4. Diagrams:**

*   **Figure 4: Instantaneous CH* chemiluminescence image**
    *   Components:
        *   Dump plane
        *   Centerbody

**5. Key Numbers, Constants, Parameters:**

*   Modulation frequency (Figure 3): 100 Hz
*   Inlet Temperature (Tin): 200°C
*   Mean Velocity (Vmean) : 60 m/s
*   Mean equivalence ratio (Φmean): 0.60
*   Equivalence ratio fluctuation: 4.3%
*   ICCD camera gate width: 0.833 ms, corresponds to Tdcs/12
*   Modulation frequency (Figure 4): 100 Hz
*   Mean equivalence ratio (Φmean): 0.55
*   Modulation frequency (Figure 5): 150 Hz

title: Experimental_Investigation_of_the_Nonlinear_Response_of_Swirl-Stabilized_Flames_
text:
[VISUAL/EQUATION CONTENT - Page 6]
Here's the technical content extracted from the provided text and images:

**1. Mathematical Equations:**

*   Δφ = τ(2πf)
*   Lflame ~ S_T^-1 (S_T = turbulent flame speed)

**2. Graphs/Charts:**

*   **Figure 8:**
    *   Axes:
        *   X-axis: Φ'/Φmean, unitless
        *   Y-axis: CH*/CH*mean, unitless
    *   Curves: Scattered plot of data showing distinct grouping
        *   Φ = blowoff (equivalence ratio)
        *   Φ = 0.60
        *   Φ = 0.65
        *   Φ'ΔΦmean,10% extended

*   **Figure 9(A):**
    *   Axes:
        *   X-axis: Frequency (Hz)
        *   Y-axis: G (Gain), unitless
    *   Curves: Gain vs Frequency curve. Key values: Peak gain around 175Hz.

*   **Figure 9(B):**
    *   Axes:
        *   X-axis: Frequency (Hz)
        *   Y-axis: Phase (rad)
    *   Curves: Phase vs Frequency. Linear relationship indicated.

**3. Tables:**
NO_VISUAL_CONTENT

**4. Diagrams:**
NO_VISUAL_CONTENT

**5. Key Numbers, Constants, Parameters:**

*   Tin = 200 °C (Inlet Temperature)
*   Vmean = 60 m/s (Mean Velocity. Also written as simply V=60 m/s in fig. 8's caption)
*   Φmean = 0.60, 0.65 (Mean Equivalence Ratio. Also simply written as Φ=0.60, 0.65)
*   Φ'/Φmean = 1% (Amplitude of equivalence ratio perturbations)
*   Modulation frequency for CH* chemiluminescence images is 200 Hz
*   Normalized Equivalence ratio fluctuation of 2.2% (a) and 3.2% (b).
*   Φ = 270 deg (Angle where CH* chemiluminescence emission is highest)
*   Modulation frequency of 150 Hz (Fig. 8 is taken at this frequency)
*   Overshoot observed at a modulation frequency of 175 Hz in Figure 9(a).

title: Experimental_Investigation_of_the_Nonlinear_Response_of_Swirl-Stabilized_Flames_
text:
[VISUAL/EQUATION CONTENT - Page 7]
Here's the technical content extracted from the provided text:

**1. Mathematical Equations:**

*   No explicit equations but mentions:
    *   Equivalence ratio oscillations are nearly sinusoidal.

**2. Graphs/Charts:**

*   **Figure 11: Time signal of CH* chemiluminescence intensity**
    *   X-axis: t/T (dimensionless time)
    *   Y-axis: CH* Intensity (a.u. - arbitrary units). Range: 0 to 0.8.
    *   Curve: Oscillating CH* intensity signal over time.
    *   Key values/labels: τ1, τ2 indicate time delays.
    *   Inlet conditions: Tin=200°C, Vmean=60 m/s, φ mean = 0.60.
    *   Modulation frequency = 200 Hz
    *   Φ'/φmean = 2.2%

**3. Tables:**  NO_VISUAL_CONTENT

**4. Diagrams:**

*   **Figure 10 (a & b): Phase-resolved CH* chemiluminescence imaging.**
    *   Consists of a time series (snapshots) of chemiluminescence images
    *   Images labeled with angles in degrees: 0, 30, 60, 90, 120, 150, 180, 210, 240, 270, 300, 330. (presumably degrees of a cycle)
    *   Fig 10(a): Φ'/φmean = 2.2%
    *   Fig 10(b): Φ'/φmean = 3.2%
    *   Inlet conditions: Tin=200°C, Vmean=60 m/s, φ mean = 0.60.

**5. Key Numbers, Constants, Parameters:**

*   Modulation frequency (f): 200 Hz
*   Approximate amplification frequency of flame equivalence ratio perturbations: 175 Hz.
*   Inlet Mixture Temperature (Tin): 200°C
*   Inlet Mean Velocity (Vmean): 60 m/s
*   Mean Equivalence Ratio (φmean): 0.60.
*   Φ'/φmean = 2.2% , 3.2%
*   Diameter of holes in rotor of modulation device (dhole)
*   Turbulent flame speed (ST)
*   Diameter of infrared detector (Φ)
*   Phase difference (Δφ)
*   Time Delay (T)

**Nomenclature (List of variables):**

*   A = amplitude
*   dhole = diameter of holes in rotor of modulation device
*   f = modulation frequency
*   H = flame transfer function
*   Lflame = characteristic flame length (distance from the corner of center body to maximum chemiluminescence intensity location)
*   m' = mass flow rate
*   Q = heat release from flame
*   ST = turbulent flame speed
*   V = inlet mixture velocity
*   Φ = diameter of infrared detector
*   Φ = equivalence ratio
*   Δφ = phase difference between Φ' and CH*'
*   τ = time delay
